name: Profile & Security Settings
description: View/update profile, change password, and set up/manage multi-factor authentication from within the authenticated dashboard.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:1595 (Update Password button), 1602-1607 (MFA status badge + setup body), 2798-2823 (changePassword(), calling POST /api/auth/change-password), 2825+ (MFA setup flow) — all read directly, verified 2026-07-08.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:897 sidebar nav-item (data-page="settings") reaches this page section.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs, workers/test/mfaEnforcementRegression.test.mjs, and workers/test/gdprAccountDeletion.test.mjs import directly from workers/src/handlers/auth.js — confirmed by import-statement grep, 2026-07-08.

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:1595-1607,2798-2825 read directly; workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs and workers/test/mfaEnforcementRegression.test.mjs import statements confirmed.

notes: No actionable gap found in this pass. P7 assigned by elimination (no P1-P6 condition applies).

name: Scan History & Reports
description: View past scans/purchased reports with expiry and re-download.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:3700-3722 (loadUserReports()) renders the reports table with module/target/price/expiry/download columns and is wired to fire on nav to the reports page or on load if already active. As of commit 67f6b924 (PR #97 quick-win fix), this function is the sole owner of #reports-table — the previous race condition against loadPayments() (both functions writing the same DOM element, whichever resolved last silently won) was removed by scoping loadPayments() to #payment-history only. Independently re-confirmed 2026-07-08: no other function in the file references #reports-table.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/billingPortal.test.mjs and workers/test/commercialPlatform.test.mjs reference report_url/customer payments behavior (confirmed by grep for these terms; both test the payments/billing data shape this page consumes).

verification.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:3700-3722 read directly and cross-checked against the PR #97 diff (git show 67f6b924) to confirm the previously-documented race condition is actually fixed in current code, not just claimed fixed.

notes: This entry supersedes docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:227's 'Exists, buggy' status — the bug it references (Quick Win #3) was fixed in commit 67f6b924, after the mission brief was written. No actionable gap found now; P7 by elimination.

name: Session Management (Active Sessions / Per-Session Revoke)
description: Let a customer see which devices/sessions are currently signed in and revoke an individual session, rather than only being able to sign out everywhere at once.

frontend.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-10 (production-readiness lifecycle wave 1). New 'Active Sessions' card added to #page-settings between Change Password and 2FA, table body #sessions-list. loadSessions() (lazy-loaded the first time Settings is opened, same pattern as loadMFAStatus()) calls GET /api/user/sessions with an X-Session-Hint header carrying the browser's own already-held refresh token (sessionStorage 'cdb_refresh') purely so the backend can flag which row is 'This device' — not a new privilege, the token is already held client-side and already usable at /api/auth/refresh. renderSessions() shows a device label parsed from user_agent (sessDeviceLabel()), IP, signed-in date, and a per-row 'Sign out' button (hidden for the current session) wired via a data-session-id attribute + revokeSession(this.dataset.sessionId) — not a string-interpolated onclick, deliberately following the CAP-ADMIN-004 fix's safer pattern even though session ids are server-generated hex (no actual injection vector) rather than the older, riskier convention.

navigation.evidence: Embedded in the existing, already-discoverable Settings page (data-page="settings"), same sidebar nav-item as Change Password / 2FA.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/userSessionManagement.test.mjs (new, 2026-07-10, 13 tests, real in-memory node:sqlite matching production schema, same convention as workers/test/phase10PasswordReset.test.mjs): lists only the caller's own active non-revoked non-expired sessions, flags the current session via X-Session-Hint without leaking token_hash to the client, returns the device/IP/created_at fields the UI renders, requires authentication on both routes, revokes an owned session, 404s (not silently succeeds) on another user's session id (IDOR-safe) and on an already-revoked/nonexistent id, plus a route+frontend contract check (workers/src/index.js registers both routes wired to the real handlers; frontend/user-dashboard.html calls the real endpoints, not a placeholder; the revoke button uses a data-attribute, not a string-interpolated onclick).

verification.evidence: FIXED 2026-07-10. workers/src/handlers/auth.js (handleListSessions/handleRevokeSession), workers/src/index.js (route registration + auditLog on revoke), frontend/user-dashboard.html (#page-settings card, loadSessions/renderSessions/revokeSession/sessDeviceLabel/sessEsc) all read directly. All 3 inline <script> blocks in frontend/user-dashboard.html syntax-checked clean (new Function(src)) before and after the change. Full backend suite green: 191 files / 2038 tests (190/2025 baseline + 1 new file/13 tests, zero regressions).

notes: customer_journey_complete stays false pending a real dynamic_browser (live production click-through) pass — this fix used static evidence + a real in-memory-SQL-backed handler test, not a headless-Chromium session, per this registry's own Production Truth Law (docs/ENGINEERING_STANDARDS.md §11). Deliberately minimal scope, matching the capability's own description exactly: list + individual revoke only. NOT built in this pass (disclosed, not silently skipped): a 'sign out all other sessions' bulk action (would need a new backend function that excludes the caller's own current token, distinct from the existing revokeAllUserTokens which revokes literally everything including the caller); a 'last used' timestamp (refresh_tokens has no such column — created_at is the closest available proxy and is what's shown); geo/location display (would require an IP-geolocation dependency, out of scope for this fix). Matches docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md Domain 1 (Identity) and Domain 9 (Customer Portal) rows, which both flagged this same gap before the fix.

name: Support Ticket System
description: In-product support ticket submission and tracking, as opposed to routing every support request through email.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:2720 and :4161 are the only 'support' touchpoints found, and both are static mailto: links (mailto:enterprise@cyberdudebivash.in and mailto:support@cyberdudebivash.com respectively) — no in-product ticket form, ticket list, or ticket-status API exists anywhere in the repo.

navigation.evidence: No nav entry links to a support/ticketing page because none exists; the mailto: links are inline text, not nav items.

test_coverage.evidence: No test file references a ticket/support-case handler, because none exists.

verification.evidence: Case-insensitive search for 'support ticket' and 'mailto:' across frontend/user-dashboard.html; no handler file in workers/src/handlers/ named or grepped for ticket-related exports. | CORRECTED 2026-07-14 (Enterprise Capability Completion Program triage): backend.status was 'missing' but workers/src/handlers/support.js is real -- wired at workers/src/index.js:7643-7647, implements POST /api/support/ticket with a genuine INSERT INTO support_tickets (table present in workers/schema_master.sql), plus FAQ/status/SLA endpoints. Added 2026-07-13 (git log), i.e. after this registry's prior 2026-07-08 verification -- the registry had not caught up. Also exercised via an AI-copilot tool (create_support_ticket, tested in workers/test/copilotSalesSupportTools.test.mjs). Frontend remains genuinely empty (confirmed zero matches for /api/support/, submitTicket, supportTicket anywhere in frontend/; only static mailto: links exist). Priority stays P2 -- now an accurate 'backend exists, frontend missing' gap, not a from-scratch build.

notes: Confirms docs/SAAS_PRODUCTIZATION_MISSION_BRIEF.md:229's 'Missing — only static mailto: links found' finding exactly as written; no change since that audit. SUPPORT_PLAYBOOK.md documents the operational process for handling support over email/WhatsApp, which is the current real workflow — this entry tracks the absence of in-product tooling, not an absence of any support process at all.
